scope.description: Codex-only deterministic identity cleanup prep for global corridor and route records only.
scope.author: codex
scope.selection_policy: Only high-confidence same-route aliases were queued. Route aliases require exact route_id match, same or compatible service type/variant, and no local/SBS/LTD/express/subway conflict. Corridor candidates were inspected, but no fresh corridor alias survived the exact same geography/limits guard without introducing a target-chain concern.

aliases.reason: Same Brooklyn B45 local bus route in the Flatbush Avenue bus-priority context. Both records have route_id B45 and Brooklyn borough context; the alias is explicitly typed local, while the target has no conflicting service variant and carries the same B45/B63 terminal-swap evidence.

canonical.source_quote: Potential Terminal Swap - B45 & B63; WB B45 would cross Flatbush Ave and continue along Atlantic Ave and terminate at Brooklyn Bridge Park.

alias.source_quote: Potential Terminal Swap - B45 & B63; WB B45 would cross Flatbush Ave and continue along Atlantic Ave and terminate at Brooklyn Bridge Park.

aliases.guard_notes: Route merge guard satisfied: exact route_id match, same Brooklyn context, alias local typing is compatible with target blank typing, and no SBS/LTD/express/subway conflict. Alias was absent from data/identity-overrides/merges.json and visible identity prep through identity122; pair was absent from do-not-merge.

aliases.reason: Same Brooklyn B69 local bus route in the Flatbush Avenue bus-priority context. Both records have route_id B69 and Brooklyn borough context; the target is typed local and includes the September 2025 and April 2026 B69 reroute/detour evidence, while the alias is an October 2025 B69 reroute duplicate with no conflicting variant.

canonical.source_quote: B69 Temporary Detour; B69 buses turn right onto Flatbush Av from Park Pl, traverse Grand Army Plaza, and continue along the regular route.

alias.source_quote: Potential Reroutes - B69; alternate routing prevents turning B69 from blocking B41 on Flatbush Ave.

aliases.guard_notes: Route merge guard satisfied: exact route_id match, same Brooklyn context, target local typing is compatible with alias blank typing, and no SBS/LTD/express/subway conflict. Alias was absent from data/identity-overrides/merges.json and visible identity prep through identity122; pair was absent from do-not-merge.

aliases.reason: Same Metro-North Hudson Line route. Both records have route_id Hudson Line and route_type_normalized commuter_rail; the target aggregates multiple Metro-North Hudson Line records, while the alias is a 2022 Hudson Line performance/ridership duplicate.

canonical.source_quote: Hudson Line Ridership - Commutation; Hudson Line Ridership - Non-Commutation; Total Hudson Line.

alias.source_quote: The Hudson, Harlem, and New Haven Lines performed at 97.0%, 97.3%, and 96.8% respectively.

aliases.guard_notes: Route merge guard satisfied: exact route_id match and exact commuter_rail type match. This proposal does not merge Hudson Line with Harlem Line, New Haven Line, subway routes, or bus variants. Alias was absent from data/identity-overrides/merges.json and visible identity prep through identity122; pair was absent from do-not-merge.

guard_summary.applied_merge_shape_checked: data/identity-overrides/merges.json stores alias_id -> target_id entries under aliases.<kind>.
guard_summary.visible_prior_identity_batches_checked: Visible prep artifacts run through codex-identity-batch120-prep.json; no codex-identity-batch121-prep.json or codex-identity-batch122-prep.json was visible.
exclusion_checks: Selected alias IDs had no hits as alias keys in data/identity-overrides/merges.json.
Selected alias IDs had no hits as alias_id in visible data/review_notes/codex-identity-batch*-prep.json through identity122.
Selected target/alias pairs had zero hits in data/identity-overrides/do-not-merge.json.
route_guards: Blank service typing was allowed only when paired with compatible local bus typing and no contrary local/SBS/LTD/express/subway signal.
Commuter-rail route aliases required exact route_id and exact commuter_rail type.
No local/SBS/LTD/express/subway variant conflict was included.
corridor_guards: Corridor candidates were inspected with exact street/name, limits, and borough matching.
Different limits, uncertain boroughs, broader geography, phase-specific false friends, and target-chain candidates were skipped.
No corridor alias is proposed in this batch.

commands_run.purpose: Locate applied merge overrides, visible identity prep notes, and canonical corridor/route data.
Skim the durable project log before preparing the batch.
Confirm merges.json shape as aliases.<kind> alias_id -> target_id.
Inspect the latest visible identity prep artifact schema.

commands_run.command: node read-only alias exclusion count over merges.json and data/review_notes/codex-identity-batch*-prep.json through identity122
node read-only route candidate grouping over data/canonical/routes.jsonl
commands_run.purpose: Group route records by exact route_id, borough, and compatible service type; exclude aliases already applied or prepared.

commands_run.command: node read-only corridor candidate grouping over data/canonical/corridors.jsonl
commands_run.purpose: Group corridor records by normalized corridor name/street, limits, and borough; no fresh high-confidence corridor alias was selected.

commands_run.command: node read-only selected-record inspection over data/canonical/routes.jsonl and data/canonical/corridors.jsonl
commands_run.purpose: Inspect payloads, source_ids, record_aliases, and evidence_refs for selected candidates and skipped corridor candidates.

commands_run.command: rg -n selected route and block ids under raw/sources/*/blocks.jsonl
commands_run.purpose: Confirm source-block text for B45, B69, and Hudson Line evidence references.

commands_run.command: node read-only selected alias exclusion check against merges.json, visible prior identity prep through identity122, and do-not-merge.json
commands_run.purpose: Confirm selected aliases were not already applied/prepared and selected pairs were not do-not-merge pairs.

nonfatal_notes: A couple of exploratory jq count/index expressions returned errors while inspecting object shape; they did not modify files and were replaced by read-only Node checks.
